listings.title: Systems Administrator
listings.description:
**Greenfield Engineering, a Saalex Company** is seeking a **Journeyman Systems Administrator in Patuxent River, MD** to support the Core Avionics Engineering Division (CAED) within NAWCAD.


This role supports the Avionics Engineering Department and Mission Systems Group labs, ensuring secure, reliable operation of lab environments and maintaining Authorization to Operate (ATO) compliance.

**Position Type:** Full\-Time

**Salary:** $90k\-$135k annually (depending on experience)  

**Work Location:** Full\-time onsite (see onsite requirements below)

**\*\*On\-site Requirements:** This position is not eligible for reoccurring telework, and will require an onsite presence at Patuxent River NAS for greater than or equal to 90% of the work week.

**Position Overview**


The Systems Administrator will support lab infrastructure, cybersecurity, and system integration efforts across physical and virtual environments. This role plays a key part in maintaining secure lab operations, supporting test environments, and ensuring compliance with DoD cybersecurity policies.

**Essential Functions:**

* **Administer \& Maintain Systems:** Deploy, configure, and maintain Windows/Linux systems, servers, workstations, and virtual environments (VMs/containers), including user access, system updates, and routine maintenance
* **Secure \& Harden Infrastructure:** Perform ACAS/SCAP scans, apply STIGs, patch vulnerabilities, and maintain system security posture in accordance with DoD policies
* **Support ATO \& Compliance:** Assist in creating and maintaining ATO accreditation packages (eMASS), continuous monitoring, and cybersecurity documentation
* **Troubleshoot \& Support Users**: Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, and network issues; provide Tier 1–2 support and guidance to lab users and engineers
* **Integrate \& Improve Lab Systems:** Evaluate, recommend, and integrate new hardware/software solutions into lab and test environments
* **Support Network Operations:** Maintain LAN/WAN connectivity and administer network security controls
* **Ensure Continuity \& Documentation:** Maintain backups, support disaster recovery efforts, and keep system configurations and procedures documented
* Other duties as assigned or required

  

**Requirements** **Required:**

* 3\+ years of system administration experience (lab or enterprise environment)
* CompTIA Security\+ certification (required)
* Experience with:
* + ACAS and vulnerability scanning
	+ STIG implementation and security patching
	+ Windows and/or Linux administration
	+ Network troubleshooting and system monitoring
* Experience supporting ATO/accreditation processes (eMASS preferred)
* Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to work independently

**Desired:**

* Experience in RDT\&E or lab environments
* Familiarity with:
* + Containerization (Docker/Kubernetes)
	+ DevSecOps practices
	+ GitLab
* Experience integrating hardware/software into test bench environments
* Formal OS training (Windows/Linux certifications or coursework)

**Education:**


BS/BA Degree and 3\+years of relevant experience OR AA/AS degree and 5\+ years of experience, OR HS and 7\+ years of experience.


Security Clearance:


Active Top Secret required. Requirements to obtain a clearance include US Citizenship, security investigation, etc.

 **Benefits**  

* Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental \& Vision)
* Retirement Plan (401k, IRA)
* Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary \& AD\&D)
* Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick \& Public Holidays)
* Short Term \& Long Term Disability
* Training \& Development
* Wellness Resources
* Stock Option Plan

listings.title: IT Support Specialist
listings.description:
Gridstone Holdings LLC

**Position Title:** IT Support Specialist

**Reports To:** Director of IT  
**FLSA Status:** Non\-Exempt  
**Number of Openings:** 1  
**Location:** Manchester, ME

**POSITION SUMMARY**

Gridstone Holdings LLC is seeking a motivated IT Support Specialist to provide high\-quality technical support to employees across Gridstone Holdings and its operating companies (Atlantic Power Constructors, BBC Electrical Services, Interconnect Utility Services and Reliable Substation Services). In this role, you will serve as the first point of contact for technical issues and will be responsible for diagnosing and resolving hardware, software, network, and user access issues across a growing multi\-location organization.

The ideal candidate is tech\-savvy, customer\-focused, patient, communicative, and eager to learn new technologies. This position will support day\-to\-day IT operations including troubleshooting technical issues, configuring equipment, supporting onboarding/offboarding activities, maintaining IT assets, and assisting with system maintenance and security initiatives.

**RESPONSIBILITIES**

Technical Troubleshooting \& User Support

· Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, and peripheral issues involving laptops, desktops, printers, mobile devices, docking stations, and related equipment.

· Provide technical support for Windows 10/11, macOS, Microsoft Office 365, and related business applications.

· Troubleshoot basic networking and connectivity issues including VPN access, Wi‑Fi connectivity, TCP/IP, DNS, and DHCP.

· Provide timely and professional support to end\-users in both office and remote environments.

· Explain technical concepts and solutions to non\-technical users in a clear and understandable manner.

· Escalate unresolved or advanced technical issues to senior IT personnel or vendors as appropriate.

User Account Management

· Manage user accounts, permissions, and access rights using Active Directory, Azure Active Directory (Azure AD), and Microsoft 365\.

· Support employee onboarding and offboarding activities including account setup and termination, device deployment and recovery, password resets, MFA support, and access provisioning.

· Configure laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and related IT equipment for new and existing employees.

Ticketing, Remote Support \& Documentation

· Respond to technical support requests through the Company’s ticketing system (e.g., NinjaOne), ensuring all issues are properly logged, tracked, documented, and resolved in a timely manner.

· Provide remote support using ScreenConnect, Microsoft Remote Desktop, and other remote monitoring and management tools.

· Create and maintain troubleshooting guides, IT procedures, system documentation, and basic network diagrams.

· Document recurring technical issues and assist with identifying opportunities for process improvement.

System Maintenance \& Asset Management

· Perform routine system updates, patch management, software installations, and virus/malware scans to maintain system security and stability.

· Assist with maintaining and supporting basic IT infrastructure including workstations, servers, network devices, printers, and peripherals.

· Routinely monitor and test system performance to support operational reliability and security.

· Maintain accurate inventory records for IT hardware, software licenses, mobile devices, and peripherals.

· Support overall IT operational planning, implementation, and administration activities across multiple business units and locations.

**SKILLS / REQUIREMENTS**

Required:

· 1–3 years of experience in IT support, help desk, or a similar technical role.

· Strong knowledge of Windows 10/11, macOS, and Microsoft Office 365 administration.

· Understanding of TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPN connectivity, and basic networking concepts.

· Experience with ticketing systems and remote support tools.

· Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills.

· Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

· Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and manage multiple requests in a fast\-paced environment.

· Strong customer service mindset and attention to detail.

· CompTIA A\+ certification or equivalent practical experience preferred.

· Valid driver’s license and ability to travel occasionally between company locations if needed.

Preferred:

· Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field.

· Microsoft 365 Certified: Enterprise Administrator Expert or similar certification.

· Experience with cloud infrastructure platforms such as AWS or Azure.

· Experience with endpoint management or mobile device management (MDM) platforms.

· Basic scripting knowledge using PowerShell or Bash.

· Knowledge of cybersecurity best practices, endpoint protection, and incident response procedures.

· Experience supporting multi\-location organizations or operational field environments preferred.

**WORK ENVIRONMENT**

This position primarily operates in an office environment but may occasionally require support within warehouse, operational, or field office locations. The role may involve lifting and moving computer equipment, prolonged periods of sitting or standing, and occasional travel between company locations.

**EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER**

Gridstone Holdings is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate based on any legally protected status. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace for all employees.

Pay: $26\.50 \- $31\.50 per hour

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

**WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR**


We are seeking an experienced System Administrator with 5\+ years of hands\-on experience managing and supporting IT infrastructure in a fast\-paced environment. In this role, you will be responsible for maintaining and optimizing servers, networks, cloud environments, and security across multiple locations. You will play a key role in ensuring system performance, reliability, and security while supporting internal teams and driving continuous improvement across our technology landscape.

 **YOU COULD BE THE NEXT CHOATE SYSTEM ADMINISTRATOR IF…**

* You have managed and maintained the organization’s IT infrastructure, including servers, networks, storage, and cloud environments across multiple locations.
* You’ve leveraged best\-in\-class tools and technologies to support and optimize infrastructure performance and reliability.
* You collaborated with external vendors to monitor system health, security, and backups, ensuring continuity and protection of critical systems.
* You have troubleshooted and resolved infrastructure and end\-user issues, providing clear and effective support across the organization.
* You’ve implemented and managed system, network, and security configurations while proactively recommending upgrades, patches, and improvements.
* You support IT operations and strategic initiatives, including budgeting, hardware procurement, asset management, and enterprise system administration.
* You bring strong technical expertise in managing Windows environments and leveraging platforms such as AWS, Azure, Entra ID, Intune, Meraki, and Palo Alto to support and secure enterprise infrastructure.

One additional benefit Choate offers which cannot be understated is a **safe working environment**. Whether working in the office or at a jobsite, it is our goal to elevate safety standards and ensure everyone goes home safely – and more safety conscious – at the end of each day.

listings.title: Enterprise Systems Administrator
listings.description:
**Compensation Range:**

$66,436\.50 \- $99,645\.00
Our full\-time positions come with an array of excellent benefits including comprehensive healthcare coverage, a pension plan, flexible spending accounts and even a tuition reimbursement program.


At Lancaster County, we understand the importance of work\-life balance. That's why we offer paid time off for sick, vacation and holidays, an employee assistance program, and family medical leave.
  

  

If you're passionate about making a difference, apply today to join us in serving the community!


**Job Description:**

**JOB SUMMARY**

* The Enterprise Systems Administrator administers and protects the integrity of network\-related systems, setting up and monitoring secure environments, as well as reviewing system utilization and addressing performance issues in compliance with the County's Information Security Policy and Information Technology standards.
* The Administrator must possess a high level of experience and proficiency to interact with customer needs over the phone, through remote tools or in person. The Enterprise Systems Administrator must enter work orders and follow up on work orders in the Help Desk system. The Administrators monitors various network, application and security systems, makes recommendations for improved processes related to network infrastructure, and safeguards files through approved backups and disaster preparedness. This position supports and maintains systems and infrastructure critical to County operations, including public safety communications, 9\-1\-1 infrastructure, emergency response systems, and continuity of essential services.
* This position is deemed essential and may be required to work during inclement weather, natural disaster, other emergency situations and/or when the County offices are officially closed due to inclement weather, natural disaster, other emergency situations, etc. This position may be required to work off its normal shift.

**REPORTING RELATIONSHIPS**

* This position reports directly to the Technical Services Manager.

**ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS**

* Performs complex network administrative functions including, but not limited to, domain management, email systems, VPN, server administration, and support of systems critical to County operations and public safety communications.
* Implements security standards, policies, and procedures in accordance with County and regulatory requirements.
* Installs and supports network\-based applications and enterprise systems.
* Develops and maintains group, computer, and password policies.
* Performs security reviews and assessments for the IT department and other County agencies.
* Installs, configures, and maintains routers, switches, wireless systems, and other network infrastructure equipment.
* Creates and implements compliance rules for various information systems.
* Ensures authorized access by investigating improper access attempts and reporting violations or security concerns.
* Reviews information requests related to new programming, system implementations, infrastructure changes, or operational technology enhancements.
* Assists in maintaining the reliability and availability of infrastructure supporting essential County services, emergency operations, and continuity of government functions.
* Determines customer issues by gathering information, analyzing symptoms, troubleshooting systems, and developing resolutions.
* Follows established service level agreements, properly updates work orders, and documents work order resolutions.
* Handles problems and escalated issues using remote access tools when possible.
* Updates permissions, resets network passwords, and maintains user account information.
* Provides customers with current status updates on work orders and service requests.
* Participates in after\-hours maintenance, emergency response activities, system restoration efforts, and operational support as required.

**OTHER SPECIFIC TASKS OR DUTIES**

* Identifies, recommends, and assists with improvements to enterprise systems, endpoint management, infrastructure reliability, operational efficiency, and end\-user support processes.
* Assists with technology initiatives and operational improvements that enhance resiliency, security, and continuity of essential County services.
* Performs other duties as assigned.

**MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS**

* Four\-year degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity or related field, and a minimum of five (5\) years experience in Information Technology field or an equivalent combination of acceptable training and experience which has provided the required knowledge, skills, ability, education, and experience adding up to nine (9\) years.
* These qualifications will be included in the job posting and will be used to screen applicants.

**KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES**

* Applicable knowledge of and ability to complete job responsibilities under privacy guidelines such as the County Information Security Policy, HIPAA and CJIS.
* Understanding of the importance of maintaining secure, reliable, and highly available systems which support essential County operations and public safety functions.
* Ability to maintain a strong customer service focus while providing technical support and IT services.
* Working knowledge of Microsoft desktop operating systems, Microsoft Office products (Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint); and possess the computer skills to learn software programs quickly.
* Familiarity with Active Directory Users and Computers tools.
* Familiarity with Microsoft System Center operations, reporting, and compliancy tools
* Familiarity with VMware virtualization technologies and server infrastructure concepts.
* Familiarity with networking concepts, remote access technologies, endpoint security practices, and enterprise infrastructure support.
* Ability to perform a variety of tasks; possess strong organizational skills and attention to details skills; and the ability to maintain flexibility to multitask as needed.
* Ability to communicate effectively with customers, peers, and policy\-making bodies within the County.
* Demonstrates professionalism in both appearanceand interpersonal interactions with end users, stakeholders, and IT personnel.

**REQUIRED LICENSES/CERTIFICATIONS/CLEARANCES**

* Certifications desired, but not required: CompTIA Network\+, CompTIA Security\+
* A valid driver’s license and criminal background check are required

**PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS/WORK ENVIRONMENT**

* Operate standard office equipment
* Sit long periods of time in an office environment
* Lift a minimum of 50 pounds
* Frequently move computers and equipment
* Frequent travel to various County\-owned and leased facilities

The County of Lancaster offers comprehensive benefits to our employees. Read more about our benefits here .
  

  

Lancaster County Government provides Equal Employment Opportunity for all persons regardless of race, religion, age, sex, national origin, genetic information or disability. The County also observes all applicable laws regarding Veterans status. The County reflects this action in all areas of employment and compensation practices and policies. Employment with the County is based upon the ability to perform the job as well as dependability and reliability once an individual is hired.

**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Must be able to remain in a stationary position at least 50% of the time.
* Needs to occasionally move about inside the office to access file cabinets, office machinery, etc.
* Constantly operate a computer and other office productivity machinery, such as, the copy machine/fax machine/computer/printer.

listings.title: Jr. Network and Systems Administrator
listings.description:
**Overview**

Cocanougher Asset Management, LLC (CAM), is seeking a Junior IT Network and Systems Administrator to support technology operations across CAM entities. This individual will work under the direction of the Senior IT Network and Systems Administrator to assist with network infrastructure, systems administration, endpoint support, user support, documentation, and day\-to\-day troubleshooting.

This is a hands\-on, on\-site role based out of the North Richland Hills office, supporting multiple business locations and technology environments. Travel to other CAM entities may be required, including locations 2\+ hours from the corporate office.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Network \& Infrastructure Support**

· Assist with managing and maintaining wired, wireless, and guest network environments.

· Troubleshoot internet, Wi\-Fi, LAN/WAN, DHCP, DNS, VLAN, firewall, and general connectivity issues.

· Monitor network performance and escalate larger issues as needed.

· Work with internet service providers, vendors, and internal staff to resolve connectivity and infrastructure issues.

· Help maintain network documentation, topology maps, hardware inventory, and configuration notes.

**Systems Administration \& Endpoint Support**

· Support Windows computers, servers, printers, network equipment, and other business technology.

· Assist with user account setup, changes, and deactivation.

· Prepare, configure, and redeploy computers for new and existing employees.

· Assist with patch management, software updates, endpoint protection, antivirus enrollment, and device compliance.

· Monitor backups, system health, and endpoint status as directed.

· Troubleshoot hardware, software, operating system, printer, and conference room issues

· Document issues, fixes, procedures, and recurring problems.

· POS/payment systems may be supported as needed.

**User Support \& Troubleshooting**

· Provide hands\-on support for employees across CAM entities.

· Research unfamiliar technical issues and work through problems to resolution.

· Communicate clearly with users and provide updates while issues are being worked on.

· Complete tasks assigned by the Senior IT Network and Systems Administrator.

· Escalate issues when needed with clear notes, troubleshooting steps taken, and relevant details.

**Qualifications**

* 1–3 years of hands\-on IT support, helpdesk, networking, or systems administration experience preferred.
* High school diploma required; associate’s degree in IT, Computer Science, or related field preferred.
* Certifications such as CompTIA A\+, Network\+, Security\+, or Ubiquiti\-related certifications are a plus.
* Understanding of networking fundamentals including LAN/WAN, DHCP, DNS, VLANs, firewalls, Wi\-Fi, and internet troubleshooting.
* Experience supporting Windows environments, computers, printers, routers, access points, and networked devices.
* Familiarity with Microsoft 365, endpoint protection, patching, backups, and remote support tools preferred.
* Strong troubleshooting, documentation, and communication skills.
* Ability to research and resolve unfamiliar technical issues.
* Comfortable working independently and taking ownership of IT issues through resolution.
* Ability to balance daily support needs with assigned projects and longer\-term IT tasks.
* Willingness to travel to other CAM entities as needed, including locations 2\+ hours away.

**Preferred Skills**

* Familiarity with POS/payment systems such as Toast, Square, Stripe, or similar platforms.
* Experience with Ubiquiti/UniFi networking equipment.
* Experience with Microsoft Intune, Entra ID, Google Workspace, or cloud\-based administration.
* Basic server monitoring or server administration experience.
* Experience providing remote troubleshooting support.

**Employment Type \& Schedule**

* Full\-time, on\-site.
* Standard schedule Monday–Friday.
* Occasional after\-hours, weekend, or travel support may be required depending on business needs.

**About Cocanougher Asset Management, LLC**

Cocanougher Asset Management oversees technology, operations, and strategic growth across a diverse portfolio including hospitality, energy, and property management ventures. This role supports CAM entities by helping maintain reliable technology systems, improving user support, and assisting with continued digital transformation across the organization.

**Location:** North Richland Hills, TX, with travel to other CAM entities as needed  
**Reports To:** Senior IT Network and Systems Administrator / Director of Technology

Pay: $48,000\.00 \- $68,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

listings.title: IT Support Specialist
listings.description:
**Requires successful post\-offer completion of a background investigation, physical exam, and drug test (including marijuana/THC) as a condition of employment with the City of Harrisonville.**

**General Definition of Work**


IT Support Specialist plays a crucial role in maintaining the efficiency and functionality of the city’s IT infrastructure. This position acts as the first point of contact for technical support, addressing and resolving a variety of IT\-related issues. The specialist is responsible for managing helpdesk tickets, providing timely and effective solutions to hardware and software problems, and ensuring the seamless operation of printers and related equipment. In addition to technical troubleshooting, the IT Support Specialist delivers outstanding customer service by assisting municipal staff with their IT needs and providing clear communication throughout the support process. The role also involves documenting support requests, maintaining accurate records, and contributing to the development of user guides and technical documentation.


Work is performed under the supervision of IT Director

 **Essential Functions**

*To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential function satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.*

* Respond to helpdesk calls and tickets, providing first\-level support to resolve technical issues.
* Document and track the status of all helpdesk requests and follow up to ensure timely resolution.
* Diagnose and resolve hardware and software problems, including desktop computers, and laptops.
* Perform basic network troubleshooting and escalate complex issues to higher\-level support when necessary.
* Install, configure, and maintain printers and related equipment.
* Provide excellent customer service to all staff, ensuring clear communication and a helpful attitude.
* Educate users on basic IT practices and self\-service options to improve overall IT literacy.
* Troubleshoot and resolve printer\-related issues.
* Maintain accurate records of support requests, solutions, and equipment inventory.
* Develop and update user guides and technical documentation as needed
* Assist with the set\-up and deployment of new computers and IT equipment.
* Participate in ongoing training and professional development to stay current with technology trends and best practices.
* Performs additional duties as required.
* Follow city and department policies and procedures.
**Minimum Qualifications**

* High School diploma
* Familiarity with basic computer hardware and software troubleshooting.
* Proficiency with Microsoft and basic understanding of operating systems (Windows, Mac OS).
* CompTIA A\+ Certification or equivalent.
**Special Qualifications**

* A valid driver’s license upon hire.
* Strong problem\-solving skills and attention to detail.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

**Starting pay $21\.26 per hour, up to $34\.01 per hour plus a generous benefit package.**

* Medical/Dental/Vision \- Some plans 100% paid by the city.
* Five weeks of Paid Time Off (PTO) \- accrued throughout the year.
* Life insurance ($50,000\) paid by the city.
* Long\-term disability paid by the city.
* Deferred compensation plan \- not matched by the city.
* Cafeteria plan \- health care reimbursement; dependent care reimbursement; premium savings
* Free membership to the Harrisonville Community Center for the employee \& discounted rates for family members
* Longevity pay \- starting at 10 years of service.
* Bi\-weekly pay periods
* Paid Holidays
* Comp Time
* Performance reviews with possible salary increases given after a six\-month introductory period, then annually the beginning of the calendar year.
* Retirement (LAGERS) paid by the city.
	+ Missouri LAGERS – A Secure Retirement for All (molagers.org)
* This position is open until filled.


The City of Harrisonville is an equal\-opportunity employer.

responsibilities: Respond to helpdesk calls and tickets
Diagnose and resolve hardware and software problems

listings.title: IT Support Specialist
listings.description:
**Date:** May 20, 2026


**Location:** Johnson City, TN, US, 37601


**Company:** Dentsply Sirona, Inc


**Requistion ID**: 83372  


  

Dentsply Sirona is the world’s largest manufacturer of professional dental products and technologies, with over a century of innovation and service to the dental industry and patients worldwide. Dentsply Sirona develops, manufactures, and markets a comprehensive solutions offering including dental and oral health products as well as other consumable medical devices under a strong portfolio of world class brands.  



Dentsply Sirona’s products provide innovative, high\-quality, and effective solutions to advance patient care and deliver better and safer dental care. Dentsply Sirona’s headquarter is located in Charlotte, North Carolina. The company’s shares are listed in the United States on NASDAQ under the symbol XRAY. Visit www.dentsplysirona.com for more information about Dentsply Sirona and its products.  



  

The Information Technology Support Specialist performs analytical, technical, and administrative work in the planning and installation of new and existing personal devices and workstations; diagnoses and resolves problems in response to customer reported incidents; confers with end users to determine types of hardware and software required; installs new hardware and software and maintains existing components; trains end users in the use of equipment and software; performs general maintenance tasks; troubleshoots and resolves issues with user devices and peripheral equipment located throughout the organization; prepares progress reports for all work performed; evaluates products for compatibility, expandability, and ease of use and support; recommends to management the support or nonsupport of evaluated products. Duties are performed at various levels within the defined title.  


**Responsibilities:**  


* Provide advanced customer service and technical support to end\-users for hardware and software
* Analyze, research, and resolve complex end\-user escalation incidents or issues and troubleshooting from other IT support staff; escalate as necessary
* Install, configure, and test installed hardware and software on new or existing devices, in accordance with standard procedures
* Set up new equipment and devices; set up and maintain workstations; provide appropriate access to applications, systems, and data
* Provide guidance and explain policies and procedures to IT support staff and end\-users
* Train customers on new devices and technologies
* Communicate and translate technical information to non\-technical end users; maintain communication and monitor incident through resolution
* Participate in the maintenance, implementation, troubleshooting, and end\-user support for applications, mobile devices, and servers
* Implement security policies and protocols
* Review, update, and maintain documentation to ensure internal processes and procedures, workflows, and training manuals are current
* Review and evaluate new hardware and software products; recommend the implementation of new products
* Replace equipment that has reached the end of its warranty or lifecycle
* Create, track, and maintain reports and provide updates to management
* Assist in tracking and maintaining hardware and software inventory; interact with vendors
* May monitor infrastructure issues and resolve or escalate to higher levels of support
* May mentor, train, and direct the work of other staff members and/or student workers
* Maintains full compliance with all corporate network Standards and SOPs as well as all SOX and FDA Part 11 guidelines.
* Prioritize and implement standard services
* Troubleshoot client login issues to determine and resolve the reported problem (Account, Connectivity, and Cisco VPN)
* Active Directory User Management (Creations, Terminations, Attribute Modifications, Group Memberships)
* Advanced knowledge of Microsoft M365 Products (Outlook, Office, Teams, SharePoint, Yammer, Planner, OneDrive)


Requirements:  


**Education:**
* Relevant Technical training preferred.
* Microsoft Certification preferred.

  


**Language skills**
* English \- Advanced (C1\), any other language is an advantage


**Years and Type of Experience:**
* 1\- 2 years’ relevant experience

  


**Key Required Skills, Knowledge and Capabilities:**
* Internationally and culturally aware
* Strategic focus with the ability to also operate tactically when needed
* Exceptional Analytical thinking with effective judgment and decision\-making capabilities
* Patience with users and ability to remain calm
* Strong written and verbal communication skills
* Problem\-solving and research skills that translate to the ability to find solutions for customer problems when a solution is not immediately apparent
* Interpersonal skills that function to create connections and positive experiences for customers
* Technological skills specific to the company’s products and trouble\-shooting practices

  

  

**Equal Opportunity Employer:** Dentsply Sirona is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will be considered without unlawful discrimination or regard for race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, sexual or gender identity, national or ethnic origin, age, marital status, disability, genetic factors, military and veteran status, or any other characteristics protected by applicable local law.
**Eligibility:** All successful applicants must be eligible to work in the country the position is based.
**Assistance:** If you need assistance with completing the online application due to a disability, please send an accommodation request to accommodationrequest@dentsplysirona.com.
**Agencies:** Please note that Dentsply Sirona does not accept or respond to unsolicited requests or applications submitted by Recruitment Agencies/ Search Firms.
**Notice on Fraudulent Job Offers:** Unfortunately, we are aware of third parties that pretend to represent our company offering unauthorized employment opportunities. If you think a fraudulent source is offering you a job, please have a look at the following information: careers.dentsplysirona.com.

listings.title: Learning Systems Administrator
listings.description:
**Job Description**
-------------------

**Recognized by Newsweek in 2024 and 2025 as one of America's Greatest Workplaces for Diversity**



Make Lives Better Including Your Own. If you want to work in an environment where you can become your best possible self, join us! You’ll earn more than a paycheck; you can find opportunities to grow your career through professional development, as well as ongoing programs catered to your overall health and wellness. Full suite of health insurance, life insurance and retirement plans are available and vary by employment status.



**Part and Full Time Benefits Eligibility**


* Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
* 401(k)
* Associate assistance program
* Employee discounts
* Referral program
* Early access to earned wages for hourly associates (outside of CA)
* Optional voluntary benefits including ID theft protection and pet insurance


**Full Time Only Benefits Eligibility**


* Paid Time Off
* Paid holidays
* Company provided life insurance
* Adoption benefit
* Disability (short and long term)
* Flexible Spending Accounts
* Health Savings Account
* Optional life and dependent life insurance
* Optional voluntary benefits including accident, critical illness and hospital indemnity Insurance, and legal plan
* Tuition reimbursement


Base pay in range will be determined by applicant’s skills and experience. Temporary associates are not benefits eligible but may participate in the company’s 401(k) program.



Veterans, transitioning active duty military personnel, and military spouses are encouraged to apply. To support our associates in their journey to become a U.S. citizen, Brookdale offers to advance fees for naturalization (Form N\-400\) application costs, up to $725, less applicable taxes and withholding, for qualified associates who have been with us for at least a year.



The application window is anticipated to close within 30 days of the date of the posting.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------


The Learning Systems Administrator is responsible for the end\-to\-end management, optimization, and support of Brookdale’s learning and talent systems, including the Learning Management System (LMS) and central learning portal. This role combines system administration, business analysis, and operational execution to ensure a seamless, compliant, and scalable learning experience across the organization.



The position partners cross\-functionally with Talent, HR, Operations, and IT to support system functionality, drive process improvements, ensure data integrity, and enhance reporting and analytics capabilities.



* Manage the full lifecycle of the LMS and learning portal, including system configuration, user roles, curricula, and navigation to ensure optimal usability.


* Lead system upgrades, releases, and enhancements in coordination with IT and vendors, maintaining rigorous documentation and governance standards.


* Provide Tier 1–2 technical support and deliver training to users on system functionality, reporting, and platform best practices.


* Coordinate enterprise\-wide assignments, enrollment calendars, and compliance tracking, while supporting instructors with live event production.


* Develop and distribute standard and ad hoc reports (compliance, completion, adoption) to ensure data integrity and audit readiness.


* Analyze learning data and trends to provide leadership with actionable insights and data\-driven recommendations.


* Gather business requirements for system improvements and lead UAT, regression testing, and defect tracking to ensure high\-quality deployments.


* Serve as a liaison between business stakeholders, technical teams, and external vendors to optimize system performance and resolve issues.


* Contribute to the long\-term learning technology roadmap by evaluating new tools and supporting content deployment strategies with Instructional Design teams.



*This job description represents an overview of the responsibilities for the above referenced position. It is not intended to represent a comprehensive list of responsibilities. An associate should perform all duties as assigned by his/her supervisor.*

**Qualifications**
------------------

**Education and Experience**
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience.
* 3\-5\+ years of LMS administration or systems support experience.
* Experience with reporting tools (Excel, Tableau of similar).
* Experience supporting enterprise systems and end users.


**Certifications, Licenses, and Other Special Requirements**  

None

**Physical Demands and Working Conditions**
* Standing
* Walking
* Sitting
* Use hands and fingers to handle or feel
* Reach with hands and arms
* Talk or hear
* Ability to lift: up to 25 pounds
* Vision
* Requires interaction with co\-workers, residents or vendors
* Occasional weekend, evening or night work if needed to ensure shift coverage.
* Requires Travel occasionally


Brookdale is an equal opportunity employer and a drug\-free workplace.

listings.title: Multi Function Information Systems Administrator – NASC
listings.description:
**JOB ID:** 728696BR


**Date posted:** May. 20, 2026


  

**Description:**

The Challenge:  

Crave a technically challenging position that is also rewarding? This position is for a highly technical Multi\-Function Administrator at the National ALIS Support Center, Tier 2 Service Desk. You will be responsible helping maintain fielded, operational ALIS/ODIN Nodes across the F\-35 enterprise to include the security and integrity of our systems that keep our jets flying. It takes more than steel, advanced electronics and engine thrust to make the F\-35 Lightning II, the world's fifth generation fighter, take flight. It is the Autonomic Logistics Information System (ALIS) that gives F\-35 Lightning II operators the ability to plan, to maintain, to plan and sustain its systems over the life of the air vehicle. ALIS provides the IT backbone and capabilities to support current and future Warfighters across the U.S. and allied military services.  

  

Special Requirements:
  

This position is at the F35 National ALIS Support Center supporting ALIS/ODIN. The successful candidate will serve as a Multi\-Function Info Analyst supporting US/Partner/FMS customers in multiple regions and time zones by providing problem resolution to Tier 1 Administrators sites in all facets of the F\-35 ALIS and MPE systems.
  

  

As critical members of the NASC Team, all ALIS administrators must be able to:  

* Obtain and maintain Special Access Program clearance required for program access
* Work various shift hours to include weekends to support customer mission requirements.

  

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
  

* Quickly identify root causes of system/equipment failures (hardware, software, network) and implement effective solutions.
* Communicate technical issues and solutions clearly to non\-technical users, providing guidance and training.
* Monitor system performance, test configurations, and proactively address potential problems.
* Accurately log issues, actions taken, and resolutions in ticketing systems or reports.
* Clear communication with team members, peers, and leadership.
* Ensure smooth handovers for continuity, teamwork, and preventing errors.
* Work with other teams (like IT support, field techs) to escalate and resolve complex issues.

**Basic Qualifications:**

  

* Must hold a Secret security clearance (or higher) with a recent investigation (within the past 5 years).

  

* Possess a DoD IAT Level II certification (e.g., Security\+, CySA\+, or CCNA).

  

* Experience with Database, Network, or System Administration concepts, software or hardware.

  

* debugging skills

  

* Ability to work independently, showing initiative and resourcefulness

  

* Time Management skills

  

* Working well under pressure

**Desired Skills:**

  

* Hands on experience administering F 35 ALIS or MPE.
* Technical knowledge of ALIS, including baseline hardware architecture, application to application messaging flow, and inter nodal communication.
* Experience defining and building IPsec/VPN solutions.
* Hands on experience with management of Fortigate, Watchguard, and Sonicwall
* Experience with Splunk for security log aggregation and dashboard creation.
* Experience with McAfee/Trellix products—installation, configuration, and maintenance per baseline requirements.
* Prior exposure to Service Flo (highly desirable).

**Security Clearance Statement:**

This position requires a government security clearance, you must be a US Citizen for consideration.  

**Clearance Level:**

Secret with Investigation or CV date within 5 years  

**Other Important Information You Should Know**

  

**Expression of Interest:**

By applying to this job, you are expressing interest in this position and could be considered for other career opportunities where similar skills and requirements have been identified as a match. Should this match be identified you may be contacted for this and future openings.  

**Ability to Work Remotely:**

Onsite Full\-time: The work associated with this position will be performed onsite at a designated Lockheed Martin facility.  

**Work Schedules:**

Lockheed Martin supports a variety of alternate work schedules that provide additional flexibility to our employees. Schedules range from standard 40 hours over a five day work week while others may be condensed. These condensed schedules provide employees with additional time away from the office and are in addition to our Paid Time off benefits.  

**Schedule for this Position:**

Standard Monday to Friday 40 hour work week  

**Lockheed Martin is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified candidates will be considered without regard to legally protected characteristics.**  

**The application window will close in 90 days; applicants are encouraged to apply within 5 \- 30 days of the requisition posting date in order to receive optimal consideration.**  

At Lockheed Martin, we use our passion for purposeful innovation to help keep people safe and solve the world's most complex challenges. Our people are some of the greatest minds in the industry and truly make Lockheed Martin a great place to work.
  

  

With our employees as our priority, we provide diverse career opportunities designed to propel, develop, and boost agility. Our flexible schedules, competitive pay, and comprehensive benefits enable our employees to live a healthy, fulfilling life at and outside of work. We place an emphasis on empowering our employees by fostering an inclusive environment built upon integrity and corporate responsibility.  

  

If this sounds like a culture you connect with, you’re invited to apply for this role. Or, if you are unsure whether your experience aligns with the requirements of this position, we encourage you to search on Lockheed Martin Jobs, and apply for roles that align with your qualifications.  

**Experience Level:**

Experienced Professional  

**Business Unit:**

AERONAUTICS COMPANY

Possible

Information Technology

Full\-Time  

**Shift:**

Second

**Pioneering Defense Technology:** From aerospace to outer space to cyber space, you can innovate mission solutions alongside the best minds in the business.


**United By Culture:** Excellence, integrity, and collaboration define us. We accelerate change and embrace one another’s perspectives to win for our customers.

listings.title: Systems Administrator
listings.description:
Overview:

The Systems Administrator role for Centerline Logistics works to recommend, deploy and maintain IT systems, applications, and infrastructure across all sites and vessels. This role serves as Tier 2 helpdesk, supporting technical issues and project lifecycles, as well as POC for all backend processes and applications. This specific role for the Channelview, TX location will be the primary and sole POC for the office, supported by a team based in Seattle. Some additional T1 and Fleet duties may be assigned.


Responsibilities:
* Plan and support long\-term IT strategies.
* Implement systems using industry best practices in compliance with CLL processes.
* Deploy/manage/improve hardware, software, application, and network services.
* Ensure data integrity, security, and compliance.
* Perform audits, backups, restores, and troubleshooting.
* Support application development and analyze performance.
* Recommend and implement upgrades and improvements.
* Maintain and improve documentation of processes and configurations.
* Mentor helpdesk staff and provide top\-tier support.
* Participate in emergency on\-call schedule.


Qualifications:
* Experience with Proxmox, AWS, Azure, Entra, Meraki, Sophos, Office365\.
* Experience with IIS, SQL, Veeam, SharePoint.
* Programming (ASP, TSQL, PowerShell, scripting).
* Remote and On\-Site Hardware/software troubleshooting.
* Documentation and process maintenance.
* Proficient in Microsoft products, some Linux, and enterprise networking.
* Analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Prioritize and execute tasks in high\-pressure environments.
* Strong interpersonal, oral, and written communication; customer service focus.
* Research hardware/software issues and present solutions clearly.
* Self\-motivated, detail\-oriented, and committed to continuous improvement.
* Maintain professionalism and confidentiality.


Job Condition and Environment:
* Duties are performed in a normal office environment while sitting at a desk.
* Occasional travel to vessel location and shipyards.
* May be required to travel both domestically and internationally for up to two weeks at one time.


Expected Hours of Work:
* Normal office hours are 7:00am to 4:00pm Monday through Friday
* Due to global nature of the company and scope of the role, duties may require being available periodically outside of normal working hours


Company Overview:

Centerline Logistics is a leading provider of marine transportation services in the United States. Centerline Logistics operates on the United States West Coast (including Alaska and Hawaii), United States East Coast (including Puerto Rico), the United States Gulf Coast and the Mississippi River System. Services provided include the transportation and storage of petroleum products, tanker escort, ship assist, the transportation of general cargo and rescue towing. Centerline Logistics is the parent company of eight businesses specializing in marine services and a bulk liquids terminal business. Centerline Logistics provides accounting, administrative, human resources, safety and environmental services, and other support to the subsidiary companies.  

The Company offers Medical, Dental, Vision, Life and AD\&D options to our employees and their eligible dependents after meeting Company requirements. 401(k) options, Disability Insurance, sick pay, vacation pay, 10 paid holidays per calendar year (subject to work schedule) and Healthcare and/or Dependent Care FSAs are also available to our employees after meeting Company requirements. All benefits information may be subject to change based on any current or updated benefit plan year changes and/or Collective Bargaining Agreements.  

We are an Equal Employment Opportunity and Drug Free Work place. Pre\-employment drug testing is required.

listings.title: Software Engineer (Cloud Systems Administrator), IS&T Enterprise Systems
listings.description:
The people here at Apple don’t just build products, we craft the kind of wonder that’s revolutionised entire industries. It’s the diversity of those people and their ideas that supports the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry\-leading environmental efforts. Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it.  

  

Imagine what you could do here. At Apple, new ideas have a way of becoming great products, services, and customer experiences very quickly. Bring passion and dedication to your job and there’s no telling what you could accomplish. Are you passionate about solving complex business challenges with process engineering and technology? Do you thrive in environments that require multi\-functional thinking, collaboration, and innovation? If so, this may be the job for you.  

  

**Description**  

  

We are looking for a Technical Engineer who is passionate about operational perfection through automation and engineering processes. You will play a crucial role in ensuring the flawless integration of development and operations processes, to deliver outstanding and highly available SAP systems.  

  

You will work with SAP technical and functional teams to understand infrastructure requirements, while promoting efficiency, scalability, security, and reliability throughout the software lifecycle.  

  

","responsibilities":"Collaborate with cross\-functional SAP teams to ensure applications achieve high availability and scalability.  

  

Work closely with SAP Technical teams to ensure smooth integration and deployment of on premises and GCP applications  

  

Create automation frameworks to streamline common development and operational tasks  

  

Create technical documentation, architecture designs, policies, best practices, and guidelines for the GCP Infrastructure team, while serving as an advocate for GCP Infrastructure processes  

  

Diagnose and resolve issues to ensure the continuous availability and reliability of services  

  

**Preferred Qualifications**  

  

Proficiency working with configuration management and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Ansible and Terraform  

  

In\-depth understanding of observability technologies such as Prometheus, Splunk, and Grafana.  

  

Relevant certifications in GCP (e.g., Associate Cloud Engineer, Professional Cloud Architect) are a plus.  

  

SAP Basis experience in managing and administering SAP Systems is a plus.  

  

Good organizational skills to complete and follow up with tasks and action items.  

  

Dedicated, innovative, accountable, and willing to take challenges and risks.  

  

Collaborative and through with attention to details.  

  

Adaptable to prioritizing multiple issues in a high\-pressure environment  

  

**Minimum Qualifications**  

  

2 years experience of public cloud working experience in GCP, AWS or other cloud platforms.  

  

2 years of Administrator experience in Linux or Unix.  

  

2 years of experience working in Infrastructure/IT/DevOps field.  

  

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field or equivalent years of work experience.

listings.title: IT Systems Administrator (DGF #5137)
listings.description:
Posting Details
  
  

Interviews are anticipated to be conducted within two weeks of closing date.
  
  

Our Mission
  
  

**It is the mission of New Mexico's Game and Fish Department:** "To conserve, regulate, propagate and protect the wildlife and fish within the state of New Mexico using a flexible management system that ensures sustainable use for public food supply, recreation and safety; and to provide for off\-highway motor vehicle recreation that recognizes cultural, historic, and resource values while ensuring public safety."
  
  

Please visit our website for more information at https://www.wildlife.state.nm.us/
  
Why does the job exist?
  
  

Responsible for managing the development, documentation, maintenance and security of internal and external infrastructure and technological systems, including design, configuration, installation,
  
administration, optimization, and integration. Responsible for data protection and integrity. Involved with the disaster recovery planning and execution, infrastructure design and security, as well as providing high level server, active directory, VPN, and endpoint support for the department.
  
How does it get done?
  
  

Responsible for managing the development, documentation, maintenance and security of internal and external systems, including design, configuration, installation, administration, optimization, and integration. Responsible for data protection and integrity. Design, install, configure, optimize, and maintain integrity of highly complex information systems. Develop long\-term strategic and capacity utilization plans.
  
  

1\. Design, develop, install, and maintain information systems, utilities, and applications.
  
  

2\. Resolve system emergencies with significant impact on the integrity of user data and systems.
  
  

3\. Design, configure, implement, and maintain system security strategies, policies, and procedures.
  
  

4\. Establish software/hardware standards and systems policies and procedures.
  
  

5\. Engage in long\-term strategic planning with regard to systems development and integration.
  
  

6\. Evaluate and procure tools for monitoring network and system efficiency.
  
  

7\. Conceptualize and implement systems within broad technical or structural frameworks.
  
  

8\. Perform capacity planning for system configuration, software services, network services, load distribution, and service interrelationships among computer systems.
  
  

9\. Act as a technical expert or lead for local computer system administration.
  
  

10\. Act as project leader on large\-scale computing projects in which strong technical, directional, and personal leadership is necessary; assign and oversee the work of
  
other system administrators as needed.
  
  

11\. Manage vendor relationships.
  
  

12\. Apply skill in complex multisystem platforms.
  
  

13\. Apply skill in coordinating multi\-system and computing environments in independent
  
computing facilities.
  
  

14\. Apply skill in developing/implementing a business continuation and disaster recovery plan
  
  

15\. Develop appropriate plans to meet computing needs.
  
  

16\. Lead and work on large/complex system deployment projects in a team environment.
  
  

17\. Apply knowledge of security trends and best practices.
  
Who are the customers?
  
  

Customers include department staff, peers, employees, as well as vendors, and application licensing customers when troubleshooting an in\-house development issue.
  
Ideal Candidate
  
  

Customers include department staff, peers, employees, as well as vendors, and application licensing customers when troubleshooting an in\-house development issue.
  
Minimum Qualification
  
  

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Management Information Systems (MIS), Information Technology, Engineering, or similar technical degree and two (2\) years of experience in systems administration. Any combination of education from an accredited college or university in a related field and/or direct experience in this occupation totaling six (6\) years may substitute for the required education and experience.
  
Employment Requirements
  
  

Pre\-employment background investigation is required and is conditional pending results. Must possess and maintain a valid Driver's License. Must possess and maintain a current Defensive Driving Course Certificate from the State of New Mexico or must pass and receive Defensive Driving Course Certification as a condition of continued employment.
  
Working Conditions
  
  

The work will be performed in an office environment. The person will work extended periods seated in front of a computer. The person must be able to operate a computer, keyboard, and mouse. Must be able to lift at least 25 lbs. Position requires occasional 1\) Travel 2\) Night/Weekend/holiday work 3\) Call\-back work.
  
Supplemental Information
  
  

Do you know what Total Compensation is? Click here
  
  

Matthew Alcouffe, ISD Operations Manager (505\) 629\-7894 Email
  
  

For information on Statutory Requirements for this position, click the Classification Description link on the job advertisement.
  
  

Bargaining Unit Position
  
  

This position is not covered by a collective bargaining agreement.

listings.title: IT Systems Administrator
listings.description:
**Position Summary**  

Ease is looking for an IT Systems Administrator to keep our people, tools, and infrastructure running smoothly. This is a hands\-on role at the center of how Ease operates — you will be the first point of contact for employee IT needs and the person who makes sure our SaaS environment, user accounts, and internal systems are well\-managed and properly maintained.  

  

You are comfortable working across a range of tools and requests, from provisioning new accounts and managing software licenses to configuring integrations and supporting our office environment. You are organized, reliable, and able to balance a steady queue of day\-to\-day work with the occasional deeper project that comes your way.  

  

**Position Location:** Hybrid – 3 days in Irvine office  

  

**Annual Salary Range:** $70,000 \- $85,000  

  

**What You'll Do*** **Manage user accounts and access.** Own the provisioning and deprovisioning lifecycle for all employees and contractors, including account creation, SaaS access, and SysAdmin setup across our internal and customer\-facing platforms.
* **Keep our software stack healthy and accountable.** Maintain our managed software catalog, track and reconcile licenses, evaluate new tool requests, and ensure our vendors are properly onboarded and documented.
* **Own the helpdesk queue.** Triage and resolve IT support requests across email, Slack, and our IT ticketing system. Requests range from Outlook issues and hardware setup to MFA resets and application access.
* **Administer our communication platforms.** Manage Microsoft 365 mailboxes, aliases, and routing; administer Slack workspaces including channels and permissions; and handle email deliverability configurations such as DNS records and CNAME entries.
* **Support our identity and SSO infrastructure.** Work within JumpCloud to manage users, groups, and basic SSO configurations for internal applications and customer\-facing setups, with escalation to senior IT staff for complex scenarios.
* **Maintain the office environment.** Support conference room AV and Zoom Rooms, coordinate hardware procurement and equipment tracking, and handle on\-site IT needs at our Irvine headquarters.
* **Assist with compliance and security basics.** Help gather evidence for security audits, manage employee security awareness training completions, and support IT intake for vendor assessments and tool approvals.

  

**Required Qualifications \& Skills*** 2\+ years of experience in an IT support, systems administration, or helpdesk role.
* Hands\-on experience with Microsoft 365 administration, including Exchange, Outlook, and Teams.
* Experience managing user accounts and groups in a cloud directory platform such as JumpCloud, Okta, or Azure Active Directory.
* Familiarity with SaaS license management and vendor administration across a multi\-tool environment.
* Ability to manage a ticket queue with a mix of priorities, communicate clearly with employees at all levels, and close requests thoroughly.
* Strong attention to detail when managing access, permissions, and documentation.
* Comfortable working in a fast\-moving environment where requests are varied and priorities can shift.

  

**Preferred Qualifications*** Experience with Slack administration, including workspace settings, channel management, and SSO integration.
* Experience configuring SSO for SaaS applications using SAML or OIDC.
* Familiarity with DNS records, email authentication standards (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and basic email deliverability concepts.
* Experience using a formal ITSM or ticketing platform such as Jira Service Management or Zendesk.
* Exposure to IT security basics, including familiarity with concepts like SOC 2, third\-party risk, and employee security training programs.
* Experience supporting Mac hardware in a business environment.

  

**About Ease**  

Ease.io digitally transforms plant floor audits around the world with EASE. The enterprise\-grade mobile platform combines simplicity and efficiency with powerful performance insights, helping drive quality and safety on the plant floor.  

  

Industry leaders, including Aston Martin, Dana, 3M, Tenneco, and Samsung, trust EASE to facilitate and analyze millions of plant floor audits every year. With deployments in more than 40 countries and support for more than 25 languages, EASE has established itself as a category leader in quality management.  

  

Headquartered in Irvine, California, Ease.io is a dynamic company that continues to grow as it expands its product offerings and market presence.  

  

**What We Offer**  

At Ease, we foster a culture built on respect, humility, and collaboration. We value work\-life balance as a core principle, helping ensure you can thrive both professionally and personally. And yes—we believe work should be fun, too.  

  

Growth and development are part of our DNA. We are committed to investing in your career through ongoing training, mentorship, and a clearly defined path forward. Whether you are sharpening existing skills or exploring new ones, you will have opportunities to learn and advance.  

  

We prioritize the well\-being and happiness of our team. In addition to a competitive compensation package, we offer a generous and comprehensive suite of benefits designed to support your health and peace of mind.  

  

**Here’s a look at what you can expect:*** Health Coverage: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans
* Life \& AD\&D Insurance: Financial protection for you and your loved ones
* Unlimited Paid Time Off: Time to recharge and rest when you need it
* 401(k) with Employer Match: Plan for your future with confidence through our matched retirement savings program

  

At Ease, we believe that when our employees are supported, inspired, and empowered, everyone wins.  

  

 \#LI\-Hybrid

listings.title: IT Support Specialist
listings.description:
**CRISIS CENTER OF TAMPA BAY**

**POSITION DESCRIPTION – IT Support Specialist**




**Position Details**

*Job Status:* Full Time, Non\-Exempt

*Reports to****:*** Information Systems Manager

*Department:* IT Services




**Position Summary**


The Crisis Center of Tampa Bay brings help, hope, and healing to people facing serious life challenges or trauma resulting from sexual assault or abuse, domestic violence, financial distress, substance abuse, medical emergency, suicidal thoughts, emotional, or situational problems. This role is responsible for the day\-to\-day technical support of our staff, hardware, and core business applications. While we partner with an external provider for backend infrastructure management, this position serves as the primary resource for all end\-user technical needs and local system maintenance. This position reports to the Information Systems Manager and, in his or her absence, the COO.


**Strategic/Transformational** **Duties and Responsibilities**

* Ensure the functioning of all technology systems deployed for the organization by maintaining effective communication with the different divisions of the Crisis Center, as well as working with third\-party information technology support services to minimize disruption of vital information systems.
* Involves staff and other stakeholders in the performance quality improvement (PQI) process and uses data to improve services and outcomes.

**Transactional/Administrative** **Duties and Responsibilities**

* **Identity \& Access Management:** Manage user lifecycles, group memberships, and permissions within a hybrid directory environment.
* **Endpoint Management:** Provision, deploy, and maintain workstations and mobile devices using modern automated deployment and mobile device management (MDM) platforms.
* **General Technical Support:** Troubleshoot hardware and software issues for on\-site and remote employees, including productivity suites and specialized business applications.
* **Hardware \& Peripherals:** Manage physical workstation setups, including laptop hardware, docking stations, and local/networked imaging equipment.
* **Communication Systems:** Support cloud\-based unified communications (UCaaS) and collaboration platforms.
* **Security \& Compliance:** Ensure all endpoints remain compliant with organizational security policies, including patching, encryption, and endpoint protection.
* **Documentation:** Maintain accurate hardware inventory and develop internal knowledge base articles for common technical workflows.
* **On\-Call Rotation:** Participate in a scheduled on\-call rotation to provide emergency support for our 24/7 operations.




**Required Competencies**

* **Cooperation/Teamwork** \- Works toward win/win solutions. Deals with conflict collaboratively. Discusses root causes underlying the problem. Develops effective working relationships.
* **Engaging Communication** \- Relates to people in an open, honest, sincere manner. Treats people with respect. Is friendly and approachable. Listens attentively to others. Communicates ideas clearly. Communicates appropriately with leadership and coworkers.
* **Customer Service (Internal \& External)** \- Recognizes and attends to important details with accuracy and efficiency. Treats customers courteously. Responds to customer requests in a timely manner. Elicits feedback from customers to monitor their satisfaction. Considers both short and long\-term interests of the customer in making service decisions. Proactively identifies customer needs. Takes responsibility to resolve customer complaints. Takes business or personal risks to serve customers’ long\-term interests. Creates strategies to help the organization serve customers more effectively.
* **Adaptability** \- Adapts readily to changes. Works effectively under stress. Needs minimal supervision. Comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment. Is reliable, dependable and results oriented. Maintains productivity and composure under pressure. Effectively prioritizes work and establishes clear goals and plans.
* **Problem Solving** \- Views problems as opportunities to create new solutions. Anticipates potential problems and analyzes alternative solutions.
* **Judgment** \- Gathers sufficient input before making decisions. Sees interrelatedness between issues. Considers alternative solutions. Makes appropriate and timely decisions.
* **Valuing \& Fostering Diversity** \- Demonstrates respect for individual differences and establishing a climate where all people can be comfortable and productive through sensitivity, empathy, and acceptance of cultural, racial, mental health, and socio\-economic diversity.
* **Self\-Management** – Takes responsibility for one’s behavior and well\-being; Works effectively under stress and adapting one’s style to changing situations; Comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment and needs minimal supervision; Exhibits a professional demeanor.




**Education and Experience**

* **Operating Systems:** Proficiency in supporting modern Windows desktop environments and mobile operating systems (iOS/Android).
* **Cloud Productivity:** Experience managing users within cloud\-based productivity and email ecosystems.
* **Hardware:** Ability to diagnose and perform basic repairs on professional\-grade laptop and desktop hardware.
* **Networking:** Basic understanding of TCP/IP, wireless connectivity, and remote access/VPN troubleshooting.
* **Systems Management:** Experience utilizing centralized monitoring, management tools, and ticketing systems.




**Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities**

* Knowledge of software application installation, operation, testing, and maintenance methods and techniques.
* Knowledge of current computer hardware, software characteristics and features.
* Knowledge of operating system software.
* A logical approach to troubleshooting with a desire to understand root causes.
* Knowledge of organizational standards for hardware and/or software.
* Knowledge of best practices for security (encryption, identity management, compliance etc.)
* Ability to detect, analyze, and solve hardware/software problems.
* Skill in communicating and training technical information to non\-technical users.
* Skill in systematic problem solving.
* Ability to manage time effectively and respond promptly to emergency support requests when on call.
* Comfortable working in a collaborative environment where internal IT and third\-party partners share responsibility for organizational success.
* Possess excellent time management and organizational skills.
* Ability to maintain confidential data.
* Ability to execute deliverables, work under pressure and adhere to deadlines in a manner consistent with the vision and mission of the Crisis Center of Tampa Bay.




**Physical Demands/Working Conditions**

*Physical Requirement:* Must have ability to lift and carry up to 25 lbs. Employee must be at to see, sit, climb or balance, hear, use hands, taste/smell, stoop, kneel, crouch, crawl, stand, walk, talk, and reach with hands and arms.

*Working Conditions*: Duties are performed primarily in an office setting. On occasion it will require the use of a personal automobile to travel to meetings or branch locations and you could be exposed to changing weather. The noise level is quiet.




*Travel*: Minimal. Local travel to other sites required.




*Hours*: Monday\-Friday varied hours, with on\-call responsibilities after hours and holidays.


*This job description is not intended to describe, in detail, the multitude of tasks that may be assigned but rather to give a general sense of the responsibilities and expectations of this position. As the nature of business demands change so, too, may the essential functions of this position*.




*CCTB is committed to a policy of equal employment opportunity and affirmative action for our applicants and employees. It is our policy to apply recruiting, hiring, training, promotion, compensation, and professional development practices without regard to actual or perceived race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability or certain classifications based on genetic information, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws, regulations or ordinances. Must be cleared in DCF Clearinghouse* https://info.flclearinghouse.com
